data.title: Report: ecommerce tech vendors face more tariff fallout
data.description: Bank of America Securities analysts led by Brad Sills warn that ecommerce software vendors like Shopify, BigCommerce, and Lightspeed Commerce are especially exposed.

data.title: Trump order shakes ecommerce with China tariff shift
data.description: The Trump executive order ends the de minimis exemption for goods under $800 shipped from China and Hong Kong, effective May 2. For years, that loophole allowed global platforms like Shein and Temu to flood the U.S. market with cheap products delivered directly to American consumers — all without paying duties.

data.title: Instacart adds in-stock visibility features to please consumers, hoping to help retailers as well
data.description: New Store View and Second Store Check features are meant to salvage sales, using in-stock inventory data to keep Instacart's app users — and retailers — happy.

data.title: Amazon resumes drone delivery in two test markets
data.description: The pause preceded a January Bloomberg report detailing several crashes involving the MK30 at Amazon’s drone testing facility in Pendleton, Oregon.

data.title: 'Liberation Day' tariffs threaten to reshape U.S. ecommerce
data.description: A new Baird report described the "Liberation Day tariffs" as a “reset moment” for ecommerce. It likened the ripple effects to pandemic-era supply chain disruptions, but without the same level of stimulus or consumer tailwind to soften the blow.
